Author: Andrew Murray
Title: Claude Opus 4.6 is live. And it’s free for 48 hours.
Text:
Member-only story
Claude Opus 4.6 is live. And it’s free for 48 hours.
Bolt just integrated Claude Opus 4.6.
For the next two days, paid users get it for free. Free tier users get 2x limits.
Why you should care
Most AI code tools are translators. You give instructions. They give syntax. They often miss the point.
Opus 4.6 has context.
And there are suggestions that Opus 4.6 might possess consciousness.
Huh? Well, let’s put that on the backburner for now.
Vibe Coding World is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
So what’s the deal with Opus 4.6?
It understands intent. If you say “make this responsive,” it doesn’t just paste standard CSS. It looks at your codebase. It mimics your design system. And then it matches what you already built.
This makes vibe coding more intuitive. Easier to create with.
Specifically…
The key difference to understand is contextual depth. Earlier models are like skilled translators who understand your words. Opus 4.6 is more like a collaborator who understands your intent. When you…
